page: Microsoft Azure D4_v2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv2-series, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 28.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.5 GB of RAM per core, and includes 410 GB of local storage. No GPU accelerators are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is consistently low, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests placing in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels in stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests, though it remains weak in Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 decompression. Memory read bandwidth is average at up to 551.69 GB/sec for cached blocks, while uncached write bandwidth is weak at 24.46 GB/sec.

Given its hardware profile, the D4_v2 offers moderate resource density for legacy general-purpose workloads. It performs adequately in static web serving, Redis SET operations, and database operations, all scoring in the average tier. However, it is poorly suited for compute-intensive single-threaded tasks or modern LLM text generation. The server represents a standard legacy tier where cost efficiency is tied to utilizing the bundled 410 GB local storage alongside balanced memory and multi-core resources, rather than raw processing speed.
description: Microsoft Azure D4_v2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv2-series, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores, 28.0 GB of system memory, and 410 GB of local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks reveal weak single-core processing capabilities, placing in the bottom 10% for single-threaded tasks. However, the instance achieves average multi-core performance, memory read bandwidth, and database operation throughput. This hardware profile is best suited for workloads that benefit from balanced memory-to-CPU ratios and local storage, such as static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and moderate database operations, rather than compute-heavy or single-threaded applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v2 is a Dv2-series virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28 GB of RAM, and 410 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4_v2 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine with 28 GB RAM and 410 GB local storage, suited for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine with balanced memory, dedicated cores, and local storage for standard multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores
28.0 GB of system memory and 410 GB of local storage
Average performance in database operations, Redis, and static web serving
Weak single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Well-suited for general-purpose workloads and moderate database tasks
